concept: Embrace / Hold in Arms / Encircle with Body
description: To hold another in one's arms, encircling with the body in gesture of affection, comfort, or reunion. 'Lomare' is full-body communication—the hug that says what words cannot, the physical expression of care that transcends language.
sound_symbolism: The word encircles like arms embracing. 'Lo' reaches with liquid openness, arms extending. Then 'ma' meets with nasal warmth, bodies coming together. Finally 're' receives, the embrace complete, both held.
grammatical_notes: As verb: 'mi thu lomare' (I embrace you). As noun: 'wela lomare' (good embrace). Implies mutual participation; forced holding would use different vocabulary. Related to palo (touch) but specifically the encircling hold.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Embrace communicates acceptance and care beyond words—the held one knows they are valued without speech.
pillars.pre-industrial-wisdom: Traditional greetings and farewells often include embrace—bodies confirming what hearts feel.
